rules.name: Kebele/ID Mandate
rules.description: No freelancer account shall be activated without the upload of a valid government-issued ID (Kebele ID, Passport, or Digital ID/Fayda). The name on the profile must exactly match the name on the ID.

rules.name: Residency Requirement
rules.description: Freelancers must provide a primary location within the recognized sub-cities of Addis Ababa. Profiles registered outside the metropolitan area will be flagged or restricted.

rules.name: Student Verification Priority
rules.description: Users uploading a valid University Student ID alongside their Kebele ID will receive a 'Student Freelancer' badge, allowing for specific work-hour filtering.

rules.name: One Person, One Profile
rules.description: A unique phone number and ID combination can only be associated with one account. Duplicate accounts detected via ID matching will be permanently banned.

rules.name: The '5-Active' Rule
rules.description: To prevent job hoarding, a freelancer may have a maximum of 5 active applications (pending status) at any one time.

rules.name: Concurrent Job Limit
rules.description: A freelancer is restricted to 2 'In-Progress' gigs simultaneously to ensure quality and academic balance for students.

rules.name: Re-Application Lock
rules.description: If a Client rejects an application for a specific gig, the freelancer is blocked from re-applying to that same gig ID.

rules.name: Client Posting Velocity
rules.description: New Client accounts are limited to posting 3 open gigs per 24-hour period until they complete their first transaction.

rules.name: Self-Hiring Prohibition
rules.description: Users are prohibited from applying to their own gigs or gigs posted by accounts sharing the same device fingerprint/IP address.

rules.name: Minimum Wage Protection
rules.description: The system enforces a minimum budget floor (e.g., 100 ETB) for any posted gig to cover transport and time.

rules.name: Location Specificity
rules.description: All gigs must be pinned to a specific Woreda or Sub-City. Generic locations like 'Addis Ababa' are not permitted.

rules.name: Contact Sanitization
rules.description: Clients cannot include phone numbers or Telegram usernames in public gig descriptions. Contact details are revealed only after an application is accepted.

rules.name: Binding Digital Agreement
rules.description: The agreed budget on the platform constitutes a binding verbal contract. Deviation in final payment without mutual consent is grounds for suspension.

rules.name: Currency Standard
rules.description: All gig budgets must be conducted in Ethiopian Birr (ETB). Foreign currency offers are prohibited per National Bank regulations.

rules.name: Material Cost Separation
rules.description: Gig descriptions must explicitly state if the budget includes materials (spare parts, supplies) or if they are reimbursed separately.

rules.name: Cancellation Policy
rules.description: Clients may cancel 'Open' gigs without penalty. Canceling 'In-Progress' gigs without a valid reason impacts client completion rates.

rules.name: Review Lock-in
rules.description: Once a rating/review is submitted, it is permanent and cannot be edited or deleted by the user.

rules.name: Completion-Only Ratings
rules.description: Users can only rate gigs that have reached 'Completed' status. Canceled or rejected applications do not generate scores.

rules.name: Right to Rebuttal
rules.description: Users have the right to post one public 'Response' to any review left on their profile to explain their side of the story.

rules.name: Independent Contractor Status
rules.description: Freelancers are defined as 'Independent Contractors,' not employees of Addis GigFind. The platform is a facilitator, not an employer.

rules.name: Data Privacy & Storage
rules.description: In compliance with Ethiopian Data Protection principles, user data (IDs, phone numbers) is stored encrypted and never sold to third parties.

rules.name: Dispute Arbitration
rules.description: For disputes exceeding 5,000 ETB involving theft or damage, the platform's role is limited to providing data logs to local law enforcement.

rules.name: Platform Neutrality
rules.description: Addis GigFind does not set prices. All rates are market-driven, complying with free-market competition laws.
